visual: NEW bar graph (vertical, scale of 5, 5 categories: Yellow 20, Red 45, Green 25, Purple 15, Blue 45). Title: "Favorite Colors"
visual_2: Question appears: "How many more students chose red than chose green or yellow together?"
guide: "Last one before we check what you know."
options: [A) Add green and yellow, B) Compare red to green, C) Add all colors, D) Subtract yellow from green]
guide_2: "Green and yellow combined is 45. Now compare to red."
prompt_2: How many more students chose red than green and yellow combined?
on_correct_2: "Zero! Red is 45, combined is 45—they're equal. No difference."
on_incorrect_2: "Red is 45, combined is 45. What's the difference? 45 minus 45 is 0."
design_note: This problem has a "0" answer—students must recognize when values are equal. This prevents assumption that there's always a positive difference.
